hypothesis:
Based on the identified gap that current extraction methods fail to reliably extract SMILES representations from molecular images (as noted in the limitations), and that specialized tools like DECIMER exist but cannot be integrated due to challenges in molecular image detection and segmentation, I propose:

**Hypothesis:** A hierarchical vision-language agent that first performs layout-aware molecular structure localization using contrastive learning on chemical figure embeddings, followed by style-adaptive image preprocessing, will achieve >80% precision in end-to-end SMILES extraction from heterogeneous scientific literature figures without requiring manual image segmentation.
